ddls:
CREATE TABLE "service_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service request */,
  "client_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client making the request */,
  "client_guid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the client */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the request was made */,
  "service_guid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the service being accessed */,
  "retries_request" INTEGER /* Number of retries for the request */,
  "packets_requested" INTEGER /* Number of packets requested by the client */,
  "packets_serviced" INTEGER /* Number of packets successfully serviced */,
  "max_hole_size" INTEGER /* The largest gap in the packet sequence (max hole size) */,
  "request_status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Time taken to process the request */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Error code associated with the request */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the request */,
  "request_source" TEXT /* Source of the request (e.g., API, web portal, mobile app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_client_guid FOREIGN KEY ("client_guid") REFERENCES clients ("client_guid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_service_guid FOREIGN KEY ("service_guid") REFERENCES services ("service_guid")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_guid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the client (GUID) */,
  "client_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the client */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the client record was created */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity by the client */,
  "client_type" TEXT /* Type of the client (e.g., individual, business, government) */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the client's account (e.g., active, suspended, terminated) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the client */,
  "total_requests" INTEGER /* Total number of requests made by the client */,
  "failed_requests" INTEGER /* Number of failed requests made by the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_guid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the service (GUID) */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "service_description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the service was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the service */,
  "service_status" TEXT /* Status of the service (e.g., active, maintenance, deprecated) */,
  "service_version" TEXT /* Version of the service */,
  "service_latency" REAL /* Average latency of the service */,
  "service_bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth usage of the service */,
  "service_reliability" REAL /* Reliability of the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client making the request */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service being accessed */,
  "log_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message or details of the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "request_duration" REAL /* Duration of the request processing */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of retries for the request */,
  "packet_loss" REAL /* Packet loss rate for the request */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., debug, info, warning, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)
